chunks.text: Michael Luby named 2015 ACM Fellow - EECS at Berkeley EECS alumni Michael George Luby (CS Ph.D. ’83), VP of Technology at Qualcomm is among the class of 2015 ACM (Association for Computing Machinery) Fellows . In 1996-1997, while at ICSI (International Computer Science Institute) he led the team that invented Tornado codes. He is recognized for his contributions to coding theory, cryptography, parallel algorithms and derandomization. Categories Alumni Entrepreneurship Honors Research View Open Faculty Positions
